author: fab_10
content: <@845691100072509460> <@510888004366368794> I need a clarification, my interpretation of the engine API spec (https://github.com/jihoonsong/execution-apis/blob/focil/src/engine/bogota.md#engine_forkchoiceupdatedv5) is that the `MAX_BYTES_PER_INCLUSION_LIST` only applies to the response of `engine_getInclusionListV1` while there is no such limit when calling `engine_forkchoiceUpdatedV5`, because the CL could merge more 8KiB ILs together and so the upper bound in this case should be 16*8KiB, is that correct?

author: jih2nn
content: Yes, you can say that

author: fab_10
content:
So this Hive test needs to be changed
```
FOCIL IL bytes: oversized: sends a single-item IL whose RLP encoding exceeds MAX_BYTES_PER_INCLUSION_LIST = 8192 and expects both forkchoiceUpdatedV5 and engine_newPayloadV6 to reject it
```

author: felix314159_
content: im on it

author: fab_10
content: I just pushed the fixes that make Besu pass all the tests but that one

author: fab_10
content: Should the spec be updated to say that max size of the IL txs passed to FcU and newPayload is `16*MAX_BYTES_PER_INCLUSION_LIST`?

author: jih2nn
content: What do you think about this suggested change? <@425572898787426305>

author: felix314159_
content: the fix is pushed, the suggested change can still be discussed. i think the upper limit of `engine_forkchoiceUpdatedV5` might as well be an implementation detail (no strict tested limit of 16*8KiB)

author: m.kalinin
content: what happens if `16*MAX_BYTES_PER_INCLUSION_LIST` is exceeded?

author: jih2nn
content: It's currently undefined. For context, the 8 KiB limit was introduced to cap the bandwidth consumption.

author: m.kalinin
content: if there are no constraints from the EL side then it seems no reason to introduce limitation in this case, these 16 ILs is only between CL and EL, right?

author: jih2nn
content: Yes, as long as the number of IL committee members per slot remains 16.

author: jih2nn
content: I'm on my way to the airport but will take a closer look into your PR next week. Feel free to merge it first, if you'd prefer. Thanks for great work!

author: m.kalinin
content: I don't think there should be a limit for fcU and newPayload, at least don't see any reason for it rn

author: fab_10
content: So on the EL side there will be no limit on ILs size on both  FcU and newPayload, since it is a CL responsibility to check and enforce the limits

author: jih2nn
content: Got it, thanks! What do you think about getInclusionList? (CL will ignore any ILs exceed 8KiB limit so there will be no consensus issue though.)

author: m.kalinin
content: i think the spec says that the size must not exceed that limit right? which is reasonable in this context

author: jih2nn
content: Exactly. I know you reviewed it while back but just wanted to re-check if anything can be improved. Thanks!

author: felix314159_
content:
<@281967554572976128>  ethrex fails two tests now, feel free to look into it when u have the time:

* `engine_forkchoiceUpdatedV5 accepts IL larger than MAX_BYTES_PER_INCLUSION_LIST (ethrex_default)`
* `engine_newPayloadV6 returns VALID when payload includes the appendable IL tx (ethrex_default)`

besu passes all. thx for the feedback!
